food_subgroup: Dumpling-based curry dish with plant-based protein substitute

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for momo curry or jackfruit-filled dumplings in curry sauce. FOODON_03315595 (dumpling food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Dumplings) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for momo curry or jackfruit-filled dumplings in curry sauce within FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific fusion dish. SNOMED CT does not have a term for momo curry or jackfruit dumpling curry.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Karana Momo Curry is approximately 280 g, comprising six steamed or pan-fried dumplings filled with young jackfruit, charred cauliflower, and onions, served in a spiced curry sauce with labneh.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Jackfruit (raw), Cauliflower (raw), Wheat dumpling wrappers, Curry sauce (generic), Labneh/strained yogurt; proportional blend estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for Karana Momo Curry in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data using approximate ingredient proportions: young jackfruit (~30%), wheat dumpling wrappers (~25%), cauliflower (~15%), onions (~10%), curry sauce (~12%), labneh (~8%). Values represent a composite per-100g estimate for the finished dish. Confidence is medium due to estimation methodology.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of steamed dumplings and wheat-based dumpling products; analogous estimate based on steamed wheat dumplings (GI ~52) from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Karana Momo Curry. GI of 52 is estimated by analogy to steamed wheat-based dumplings (GI ~52, low category) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. The jackfruit filling and curry sauce components are low-GI ingredients that do not materially elevate the overall GI above the wheat wrapper baseline. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 49.0) / 100 ≈ 25.5, rounded to 25.6. Serving carbohydrate estimated at 280 g × 0.175 = 49.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~868 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a standard serving of Karana Momo Curry provides approximately 868 mg of sodium (~38% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), primarily from the curry sauce and labneh; portion control and requesting reduced-sodium preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~25.6 per serving) driven by wheat dumpling wrappers
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that while the GI of this dish is in the low range (~52), the glycemic load per standard serving is moderate (~25.6) due to the wheat-based dumpling wrappers; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and limiting portion size to 3–4 momos can help manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content (~1.4 g per 100 g) and plant-based protein from jackfruit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Karana Momo Curry is relatively heart-friendly due to its low saturated fat content and plant-based jackfruit filling; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium intake from the curry sauce and labneh, and opt for steamed rather than pan-fried preparation to minimise added fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Karana Momo Curry (Vegetarian) is a fusion dish served at Dumpling Darlings in Singapore, combining Himalayan-style steamed or pan-fried dumplings (momos) with a plant-based filling and a spiced curry sauce. The filling is made from young jackfruit — marketed under the Karana brand, a Singapore-based plant-based meat company — combined with charred cauliflower, onions, and curry spices, encased in thin wheat-flour dumpling wrappers. The dish is finished with labneh (strained yogurt), which adds a creamy, tangy contrast to the aromatic curry sauce. Texturally, the momos are soft and yielding with a slightly chewy wrapper, while the jackfruit filling mimics a meaty, fibrous texture. The charred cauliflower contributes a smoky depth, and the curry sauce provides warmth and spice. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~118 kcal per 100 g), modest protein (~4.2 g per 100 g) from jackfruit and labneh, and dietary fibre (~2.8 g per 100 g) from the jackfruit and cauliflower. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), though the glycemic load per standard serving is moderate (~25.6) due to the wheat wrappers. Sodium content is notable (~310 mg per 100 g), warranting attention for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan due to the labneh, and contains gluten from the dumpling wrappers. It represents a creative intersection of Himalayan dumpling tradition, South Asian curry flavours, and Singapore's plant-based food innovation scene.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Karana Momo Curry?
What is labneh in momo curry?
Is Karana Momo Curry suitable for diabetics?
Where can I get Karana Momo Curry in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Karana Momo Curry exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data using proportional ingredient blending. GI is estimated by analogy to steamed wheat dumplings from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient list is clearly specified by the restaurant and the USDA component data is high quality.
notes: 1. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source data for the component ingredients of this dish at a level of precision sufficient for a reliable composite estimate; this sentinel value is used per the Veritas numeric hygiene rule for unreported nutrients. 2. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component ingredients in USDA FoodData Central at the level of precision required for a composite estimate; this sentinel value is used per the Veritas numeric hygiene rule for unreported nutrients. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.8 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.6 g) are estimated based on the remaining total fat after accounting for saturated fat, distributed proportionally based on typical vegetable oil and dairy fat profiles in similar dishes, using USDA FoodData Central component data. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; this is confirmed zero as the dish uses plant-based ingredients and no hydrogenated fats are present in the described recipe. 5. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on steamed wheat dumplings from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 6. The cuisine tag 'Chinese' in the input record is considered a platform categorisation artefact; the dish is more accurately described as Himalayan-Singaporean fusion, as momos are of Himalayan (Tibetan/Nepali) origin and the restaurant operates in Singapore. 7. Labneh contains dairy; the dish is therefore vegetarian but not vegan. 8. Allergen information is based on described ingredients; actual allergen status should be confirmed with the restaurant. 9. Potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and carry medium confidence.
